mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of monetary tightening, or the signal predictive power reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflationary pressures force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 235.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns when payrolls grow but unemployment also rises, or the interaction effect shows a negative coefficient in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production coinciding with elevated consumer prices signals stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy in walk-forward tests.
